title: Invalid mount documentation for libpod/containers/create
body:
### Issue Description

I get an error saying `container directory cannot be empty` which is a sign of the target directory being empty for my mounts when I try to call `libpod/containers/create`. I use the [documentation](https://docs.podman.io/en/stable/_static/api.html#tag/containers/operation/ContainerCreateLibpod) for mounts which states the following JSON for mounts:
```json
"mounts": [
  {
    "BindOptions": {},
    "ClusterOptions": { },
    "Consistency": "string",
    "ReadOnly": true,
    "Source": "string",
    "Target": "string",
    "TmpfsOptions": {},
    "Type": "string",
    "VolumeOptions": {
      "DriverConfig": {
        "Name": "string",
        "Options": {
            "property1": "string",
            "property2": "string"
        }
      },
      "Labels": {},
      "NoCopy": true,
      "Subpath": "string"
    }
  }
],
```

However the JSON request seems to get decoded in `pkg/api/handlers/libpod/containers_create.go` to a `specgen.SpecGenerator` instance and `Mounts` in `./pkg/specgen/specgen.go` refers to `[]spec.Mount` from `vendor/github.com/opencontainers/runtime-spec/specs-go/config.go` which contains the following fields:
```go
	// Destination is the absolute path where the mount will be placed in the container.
	Destination string `json:"destination"`
	// Type specifies the mount kind.
	Type string `json:"type,omitempty" platform:"linux,solaris,zos"`
	// Source specifies the source path of the mount.
	Source string `json:"source,omitempty"`
	// Options are fstab style mount options.
	Options []string `json:"options,omitempty"`

	// UID/GID mappings used for changing file owners w/o calling chown, fs should support it.
	// Every mount point could have its own mapping.
	UIDMappings []LinuxIDMapping `json:"uidMappings,omitempty" platform:"linux"`
	GIDMappings []LinuxIDMapping `json:"gidMappings,omitempty" platform:"linux"`
```

These fields does not match the fields in the documentation. Have I misunderstood something or are there discrepancies in the object mapping from
which swagger generates its documentation?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `systemctl --user start podman.socket`
2. 
```sh
curl -XPOST \
	--unix-socket /run/user/$(id -u)/podman/podman.sock \
	-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
	-d'{"command":["echo foo"],"image":"debian:latest","mounts":[{"target":"/tmp","source":"/opt","type":"bind"}],"name":"podman-test"}' \
	http://d/v5.0.0/libpod/containers/create
```
3. Get error `{"cause":"container directory cannot be empty","message":"container directory cannot be empty","response":500}`
4. 
```sh
curl -XPOST \
	--unix-socket /run/user/$(id -u)/podman/podman.sock \
	-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
	-d'{"command":["echo foo"],"image":"debian:latest","mounts":[{"destination":"/tmp","source":"/opt","type":"bind"}],"name":"podman-test"}' \
	http://d/v5.0.0/libpod/containers/create
```
5. Successfully create container with response `{"Id":"a4bef09d50a68ff0fcc61eba757ed3defa2ac2d3a9ad7bc20802f3009c00ee56","Warnings":[]}`

### Describe the results you received

I am getting an error `{"cause":"container directory cannot be empty","message":"container directory cannot be empty","response":500}`

### Describe the results you expected

I expect to successfully create a container and a response similar to `{"Id":"a4bef09d50a68ff0fcc61eba757ed3defa2ac2d3a9ad7bc20802f3009c00ee56","Warnings":[]}`

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I run a developer build of podman v5.3.0 because I added additional logging to help me debug the issue.

### Additional information

Seems like there are similar issues:
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13092
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13717

comments.body: The swagger generation is buggy so quite a few fields are documented incorrectly. Basically and struct which has the same name as another gets overwritten and only one is included in the final swagger so for all types with name conflicts we have this issue.

Swagger seems buggy in other regards like generating working stubs for Rust that will compile. Are there any thoughts on moving over to OpenAPI? From what I've understood the specifications have diverged enough for them to not be fully compliant.
